heading: Sales: members of the naval service and Coast Guard; widows and widowers; civilian employees and other persons
label: 8801 Sales: members of the naval service and Coast Guard; widows and widowers; civilian employees and other persons
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7601(a).34 U.S.C. 533, (1st proviso); , ; , ; , ; , ; , .Mar. 3, 1909, ch. 25535 Stat. 768Apr. 14, 1937, ch. 7850 Stat. 63June 10, 1939, ch. 19653 Stat. 814Jan. 23, 1942, ch. 1556 Stat. 13Apr. 9, 1943, ch. 3957 Stat. 60Aug. 8, 1953, ch. 390, § 267 Stat. 4997601(b).34 U.S.C. 533a(as applicable to Secretary of the Navy), .Aug. 8, 1953, ch. 390, § 167 Stat. 499In subsection (a) the words “members of the naval service” are substituted for the words “officers and enlisted men of the Navy, Marine Corps”.In subsection (b) the word “outside” is substituted for the words “beyond the continental limitations”. The words “or in Alaska” are omitted, since, in , the words, “United States” are defined to include only the States and the District of Columbia. The word “continental”, after the words “within the”, is omitted for the same reason. The last sentence is substituted for (proviso).section 101(1) of this title34 U.S.C. 533a

children.label: a
children.text: Such stores as the Secretary of the Navy designates may be procured and sold to members of the naval service, members of the Coast Guard, and widows and widowers of such members.
at military installations outside the United States; and

at military installations inside the United States where he determines that it is impracticable for those civilian officers, employees, and persons to obtain those stores from private agencies without impairing the efficient operation of naval activities.
